title: Organizing study repos using Barista
text: vignette describes can use Barista package

title: Organizing study repos using Barista
text: Barista package currently maintained Github repository, dependencies packages Github. packages can downloaded installed within R using drat package: installed, can type library(Barista) load package.

title: Organizing study repos using Barista
text: Considerable work dedicated provide Barista package. , Barista makes extensive use Ulysses package CohortGenerator package.

code: @Manual{,   title = {Barista: Barista},   author = {Ajit Londhe and Martin Lavallee and Katy Sadowski},   year = {2025},   note = {R package version 0.0.1, https://github.com/OHDSI/Barista},   url = {https://ohdsi.github.io/Barista/}, }

title: Barista
text: purpose Barista provide streamlined, standard way create manage study assets needed throughout life observational study conducted using RWD transformed OMOP Common Data Model OHDSI tools.

title: Barista
text: prior OHDSI studies, researchers built cohort concept set manifests track assets study. context, manifest merely table assets metadata needed study. manifest manually maintained CSV file, brought organization transparency improvements. However, manual edits CSV needed challenging study lead, id management cumbersome maintain accurately.

title: Barista
text: avoid manual CSV edits, Barista built upon Sqlite manifest system. allows us track concept sets, cohorts, files, analyses, tags robust manner. database SQLite, object can stored disk without setup, can tracked version control (e.g. Git).

title: Barista
text: Based upon prior studies, observed ids cohorts concept sets largely arbitrary - just need consistent unique. Therefore, Barista uses built-benefits SQLite database automatically generated ids manifest table. means can add new items manifest without worrying id management, ids unique consistent.

title: Barista
text: Barista built support Ulysses lifecycle, set best practices managing RWD studies. includes idea analysis tasks, migration tasks, dissemination tasks support linear lifecycle study.

title: Barista
text: cohorts, Barista integrates CohortGenerator package producing Cohort Definition Set can leveraged CohortGenerator’s functions.

title: Barista
text: observational studies, often many versions concept sets cohort definitions, either due ongoing development work, due specific needs endpoints within study. ensure metadata given manifest item tracked transparent, Barista offers TagManifest, can used create 1--many relationship manifest item tag values.

title: Barista
code: # First construct a ManifestDb object  Barista::createManifestDb(   dbPath = "/path/to/manifestdb.sqlite" )  # Create a Concept Set Manifest Item  thisConceptSetItem <- Barista::createConceptSetManifestItem(   name = "My Concept Set",   provenanceId = "123", # this is the id of the concept set as it existed in Atlas   designMethod = "Atlas",   relativeJsonPath = "path/to/thisConceptSet.json" )  # Add it to the Concept Set Manifest  Barista::addConceptSetManifestItem(   manifestDb = manifestDb,   conceptSetManifestItem = thisConceptSetItem )  # Create a Cohort Manifest Item based on a JSON definition  thisCohortItem <- Barista::createCohortManifestItem(   name = "My Cohort",   provenanceId = "456", # this is the id of the cohort as it existed in Atlas   designMethod = "Atlas",   relativeJsonPath = "path/to/thisCohort.json" )  # Add it to the Cohort Manifest  Barista::addCohortManifestItem(   manifestDb = manifestDb,   cohortManifestItem = thisCohortItem )  # View the Cohort Definition Set   Barista::viewCohortDefinitionSet(   manifestDb = manifestDb )  # Add a tag to the Cohort Manifest Item Barista::addTagToCohortManifestItem(   manifestDb = manifestDb,   cohortId = 1,   tagName = "QC Status",   tagValue = "Complete" )
